openmeter/billing/charges/creditpurchase.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing creditpurchase.Adapter — all DB reads/writes for credit-purchase charges, credit grants, external payments, and invoiced payments. Every method honors the ctx-bound transaction via entutils.TransactingRepo and enforces namespace isolation.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every method (reads and writes)
patterns.description: Both mutating and read-only methods wrap Ent calls in entutils.TransactingRepo to participate in caller transactions and guarantee consistent reads across multi-step flows.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (creditpurchase.Charge, error) { entity, err := tx.db.ChargeCreditPurchase.Query().Where(...).Only(ctx); ... })

patterns.name: Tx / WithTx / Self triad
patterns.description: Implement Tx(ctx) via HijackTx+NewTxDriver, WithTx(ctx, tx) creating a txClient from TxDriver config, and Self(); all three are required for TransactingRepo to rebind.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx, tx) *adapter { txDb := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txDb.Client(), logger: a.logger, metaAdapter: a.metaAdapter} }

patterns.name: Config.Validate() before construction
patterns.description: New(Config) validates Client, Logger, MetaAdapter non-nil and returns an error — never construct with zero values.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (creditpurchase.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &adapter{...}, nil }

patterns.name: Namespace predicate on every query
patterns.description: Every Ent query includes a namespace WHERE predicate to enforce multi-tenant isolation.

patterns.name: withExpands for conditional edge loading
patterns.description: Edges (WithCreditGrant, WithExternalPayment, WithInvoicedPayment) load only when meta.Expands is set via withExpands — never eager-load unconditionally.
patterns.example: func withExpands(q *db.ChargeCreditPurchaseQuery, e meta.Expands) *db.ChargeCreditPurchaseQuery { if e.Has(meta.ExpandRealizations) { q = q.WithCreditGrant().WithExternalPayment().WithInvoicedPayment() }; return q }

patterns.name: Mapper functions in mapper.go
patterns.description: All Ent-to-domain mapping lives in MapChargeBaseFromDB/MapCreditPurchaseChargeFromDB; missing edges detected via lo.ErrorsAs[*entdb.NotLoadedError].

patterns.name: Bidirectional cursor pagination with sort reversal
patterns.description: ListFundedCreditActivities reverses sort order when input.Before != nil, then slices.Reverse(items) after fetching — both steps required.
patterns.example: if input.Before != nil { query = query.Order(ByGrantedAt(sql.OrderAsc()), ...); ...; slices.Reverse(items) } else { query = query.Order(ByGrantedAt(sql.OrderDesc()), ...) }

key_file_guides.watch_for: Never omit WithTx or Self — TransactingRepo requires both; MetaAdapter must not be nil.

key_file_guides.role: CRUD for ChargeCreditPurchase rows: UpdateCharge, CreateCharge, GetByID, GetByIDs, ListCharges.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateCharge calls metaAdapter.RegisterCharges inside the same TransactingRepo transaction — preserve this ordering.

key_file_guides.role: Creates ChargeCreditPurchaseCreditGrant rows linking a charge to a ledger transaction group.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GrantedAt must be stored in UTC: input.GrantedAt.In(time.UTC).

key_file_guides.role: Cursor pagination over credit grants joined to credit purchases; adapter method + package-level ListFundedCreditActivities.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Before-cursor path reverses sort order AND calls slices.Reverse(items) — omitting either breaks ordering.

key_file_guides.role: MapChargeBaseFromDB and MapCreditPurchaseChargeFromDB translate Ent rows to domain types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Check lo.ErrorsAs[*entdb.NotLoadedError] when reading edges — missing check panics instead of returning a clear error.

key_file_guides.role: Create/Update for external and invoiced payment settlement rows.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All methods wrap in TransactingRepo — never bypass even for single-row saves.

anti_patterns: Using a.db directly inside a method body instead of tx.db from TransactingRepo — falls off the caller's transaction.
Calling Ent edges without withExpands — NotLoadedError panics in the mapper.
Omitting the namespace predicate from queries — returns cross-tenant data.
Inlining Ent-to-domain mapping instead of calling Map* functions in mapper.go.
Constructing the adapter without Config.Validate() — nil-pointer panics at first use.

decisions.decision: TransactingRepo wraps every method (reads and writes).
decisions.rationale: Charge advancement mixes reads and writes across helpers; consistent reads in a caller tx prevent phantom reads between steps.

decisions.decision: ListFundedCreditActivities is also exported as a package-level function.
decisions.rationale: Tests and callers need to invoke it with a raw *db.Client without constructing a full adapter.

decisions.decision: Edge loading is gated behind meta.Expands rather than always eager-loaded.
decisions.rationale: Avoiding unnecessary joins keeps list/get queries fast; callers opt in to realizations data.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new mutating adapter method

openmeter/billing/charges/creditpurchase.purpose: Implements billing.LineEngine for the credit-purchase line type (LineEngineTypeChargeCreditPurchase). Delegates line amount calculation to rating.Service.GenerateDetailedLines and explicitly disallows progressive billing via SplitGatheringLine.

patterns.name: Full billing.LineEngine interface compliance
patterns.description: Implement all billing.LineEngine methods; unused lifecycle hooks return input.Lines unchanged or nil error. Compile-time assertions enforce the contract.

patterns.name: Config.Validate() before construction
patterns.description: New(Config) validates RatingService non-nil before constructing the Engine.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Engine, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &Engine{ratingService: config.RatingService}, nil }

patterns.name: CalculateLines delegates to rating + merge
patterns.description: Line calc calls ratingService.GenerateDetailedLines per standard line then merges via invoicecalc.MergeGeneratedDetailedLines — never computes amounts inline.

patterns.name: SplitGatheringLine always errors
patterns.description: Credit purchases are never progressively billed; SplitGatheringLine always returns an error — do not add partial-period splitting.
patterns.example: func (e *Engine) SplitGatheringLine(_ context.Context, _ billing.SplitGatheringLineInput) (billing.SplitGatheringLineResult, error) { return billing.SplitGatheringLineResult{}, fmt.Errorf("credit purchase line is not progressively billed") }

patterns.name: ChargeID required on every line
patterns.description: CalculateLines validates stdLine.ChargeID is non-nil for every line before delegating, erroring immediately if missing.
patterns.example: if stdLine.ChargeID == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("credit purchase standard line[%s]: charge id is required", stdLine.ID) }

key_file_guides.role: Entire Engine struct with Config, New, all billing.LineEngine method stubs, and CalculateLines.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChargeID must be non-nil on every StandardLine; call invoicecalc.MergeGeneratedDetailedLines after GenerateDetailedLines or detailed lines stay unattached.

anti_patterns: Implementing SplitGatheringLine with real partial-period logic — credit purchases are not progressively billed.
Computing line amounts in the engine instead of delegating to ratingService.GenerateDetailedLines.
Skipping invoicecalc.MergeGeneratedDetailedLines after GenerateDetailedLines — leaves detailed lines unattached.
Adding a lifecycle hook doing non-trivial work without checking ChargeID validity first.

decisions.decision: Engine is a thin dispatcher to rating.Service rather than computing amounts itself.
decisions.rationale: rating.Service owns all billing-period and amount calculation; the engine only orchestrates and complies with lifecycle hooks.

code_examples.scenario: CalculateLines delegating to the rating service

openmeter/billing/charges/creditpurchase.purpose: Business-logic service implementing creditpurchase.Service — orchestrates charge creation, settlement-path dispatch (promotional/external/invoice), and invoice lifecycle callbacks. Multi-step flows run inside transaction.Run; all ledger interactions go through creditpurchase.Handler.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps every service method
patterns.description: Public methods wrap adapter calls in transaction.Run / RunWithNoValue — never call adapter methods outside a transaction boundary.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (creditpurchase.ChargeWithGatheringLine, error) { charge, err := s.adapter.CreateCharge(ctx, ...); ... })

patterns.name: Settlement-type dispatch in Create
patterns.description: Create inspects charge.Intent.Settlement.Type() and dispatches to onPromotionalCreditPurchase, onExternalCreditPurchase, or the invoice path — each in its own file.
patterns.example: switch charge.Intent.Settlement.Type() { case creditpurchase.SettlementTypePromotional: charge, err = s.onPromotionalCreditPurchase(ctx, charge); case creditpurchase.SettlementTypeExternal: charge, err = s.onExternalCreditPurchase(ctx, charge) }

patterns.name: Handler mediation for ledger interactions
patterns.description: Ledger interactions are always routed through creditpurchase.Handler methods (OnCreditPurchaseInitiated, OnCreditPurchasePaymentAuthorized/Settled, OnPromotionalCreditPurchase) — never called directly.

patterns.name: BackfillAdvanceLineageSegments after non-empty TransactionGroupID
patterns.description: After a credit grant where TransactionGroupID is non-empty, call s.lineage.BackfillAdvanceLineageSegments — required for external and invoice paths.

patterns.name: input.Normalize() before Validate() in Create
patterns.description: Create calls input.Intent = input.Intent.Normalized() before input.Validate() so denormalized intents never reach the adapter.
patterns.example: input.Intent = input.Intent.Normalized(); if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { ... }

patterns.name: Config.Validate() collects nil-checks with errors.Join
patterns.description: Config.Validate() appends all missing-field errors and returns errors.Join(errs...) — never returns after the first nil field.
patterns.example: var errs []error; if c.Adapter == nil { errs = append(errs, errors.New("adapter cannot be null")) }; return errors.Join(errs...)

key_file_guides.role: Config + Validate(), New(), service struct with four required fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All four fields (Adapter, Handler, Lineage, MetaAdapter) are required; nil Handler panics on ledger calls.

key_file_guides.role: Create: normalizes intent, validates, creates DB row, dispatches to settlement path, optionally builds GatheringLine for invoice settlement.
key_file_guides.watch_for: buildInvoiceCreditPurchaseGatheringLine sets AnnotationKeyTaxable=false and AnnotationKeyReason=CreditPurchase — keep these for downstream billing correctness.
onExternalCreditPurchase may chain two state transitions; ordering must be initiate -> authorize -> settle.
PostInvoicePayment* run inside billing's PostUpdate hook (already in a transaction) — do not wrap in transaction.Run again.

key_file_guides.role: Promotional path: grants credits immediately and moves charge to StatusFinal in one step.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Checks activePromotionalCreditPurchaseStatuses to prevent double-grant on re-invocation — do not remove this guard.

key_file_guides.role: Thin delegation to adapter.ListFundedCreditActivities inside transaction.Run.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No business logic here — add logic to the adapter package-level function instead.

anti_patterns: Calling adapter methods outside transaction.Run — breaks atomicity across multi-step charge flows.
Directly calling ledger APIs instead of routing through creditpurchase.Handler — bypasses the credits.enabled=false noop guard.
Skipping lineage.BackfillAdvanceLineageSegments after a non-empty TransactionGroupID — leaves lineage records incomplete.
Adding settlement-type logic directly in Create instead of a dedicated file (promotional/external/invoice).
Omitting input.Intent.Normalized() before Validate() in Create — denormalized intents reach the adapter.

decisions.rationale: Each settlement type has distinct ledger interactions and state-machine steps; separate files avoid a switch blob and are independently testable.

decisions.decision: Ledger interactions are always mediated through creditpurchase.Handler.
decisions.rationale: Enables noop handler injection when credits are disabled without changing service code.

decisions.decision: transaction.Run is used in the service even though the adapter uses TransactingRepo.
decisions.rationale: The service atomically combines multiple adapter calls (create charge + grant + update status); TransactingRepo alone scopes only individual adapter calls.

code_examples.scenario: Service method writing to adapter and calling the handler
